Pacific Coast & Tidepools.description: San Diego's 70-mile coastline — tidepools, kelp forests, salt marshes, and coastal bluffs from the Tijuana Estuary to Torrey Pines

Coastal Sage & Mesa.description: San Diego's endangered coastal sage scrub — low aromatic shrubs on mesas and terraces, home to the California gnatcatcher

Chaparral & Canyons.description: Fire-adapted chaparral covering San Diego's inland hills and canyon systems — dense shrubland with seasonal wildflower blooms

Cuyamaca & Laguna Mountains.description: San Diego's mountain backbone — mixed conifer forests, oak woodlands, and alpine meadows from Cuyamaca Peak to Palomar Observatory

Anza-Borrego Desert.description: California's largest state park in San Diego's eastern desert — slot canyons, badlands, palm oases, and spring wildflower superbloom

San Diego River & Inland Valleys.description: San Diego's inland valleys and riparian corridors — the San Diego River watershed, San Pasqual Valley, and agricultural lands of the backcountry

South Bay & Border Lands.description: San Diego's borderlands and transition zones — where coastal, desert, and mountain ecosystems converge with unique cross-border wildlife corridors

Urban Parks & Preserves.description: San Diego's urban biodiversity hotspots — Balboa Park's 1,200 acres, Mission Trails Regional Park, and neighborhood green corridors
